title: 2. All Hope Will Leave You | The Hollywood Hack
format: Chapter 2 of Brian Raftery's 'The Hollywood Hack' opens on the December 2014 Sony leak as it spills into public view. Sony's Marisa McGrath-Liston and Michael Fisk describe promoting 'Annie' and shipping 'The Interview' while a hacker email threatened employees' families and their Social Security numbers sold on the dark web for $25. Then the emails: Amy Pascal and Scott Rudin's months-long fight over an Angelina Jolie 'Cleopatra,' Rudin's 'minimally talented spoiled brat' line, the Jennifer Lawrence and Amy Adams pay-gap revelations, and the Obama emails that pulled in Al Sharpton. Ben Fritz and journalists Matt Belloni and Andrew Wallenstein walk through what the leaked documents showed about how movies get made and killed, like a long-developed Alf film.

segments.heading: The two movies Sony had to release anyway
segments.note: the hack didn't stop the schedule

films.title: Annie
films.note: Sony's holiday family remake of the musical, with Cameron Diaz and Jamie Foxx. Publicity exec Marisa McGrath-Liston was in New York promoting it, flying in 50-plus journalists, the week the hack turned personal. An unreleased copy of the film had already leaked in the data dump.

films.title: The Interview
films.note: The Rogen-Franco comedy about assassinating Kim Jong-un, the film North Korea had denounced as 'an act of war' before the hack was even discovered. Sony held a subdued, heavily secured premiere at LA's Ace Hotel on December 11, then hosted with a Randall Park cardboard cutout, before a threat invoking 9/11 shut the press tour down.

segments.heading: The leaked slides and complaints
segments.note: the low-value stuff that leaked first

films.title: Elysium
films.note: A leaked Sony research slide warned the poverty-and-overpopulation sci-fi drama should 'avoid sociopolitical themes.'

films.title: Grown Ups 2
films.note: One slide suggested a 'cool internet meme' to promote Adam Sandler's sequel; the leaked internal complaints griped about being 'saddled with mundane, formulaic Adam Sandler films.'

films.title: The Smurfs
films.note: The most-quoted slide: '58% of teens described the Smurfs' language as funny.' The franchise later became the model executives kept invoking as they debated a possible Alf movie.

segments.heading: Pascal, Rudin, and the emails
segments.note: the fight that made the headlines

films.title: Cleopatra
films.note: The Elizabeth Taylor epic name-checked as the reference point for the new Cleopatra Sony was developing: an Angelina Jolie vehicle produced by Scott Rudin. The months-long Pascal-Rudin argument over its script, budget, and even Jolie's hairstyle filled the leaked emails, and produced Rudin's 'minimally talented spoiled brat' line.

films.title: The Social Network
films.note: A Rudin-Pascal collaboration; he thanked her by name accepting his 2012 Golden Globe for it. Also the Aaron Sorkin credit the podcast notes for the irony: he wrote a movie about the internet, then called the outlets publishing the emails 'morally treasonous.'

films.title: Moneyball
films.note: Cited among the Rudin-Pascal hits still under their Sony deal in 2014.

films.title: Clueless
films.note: Listed as part of Rudin's 'impeccable IMDb.'

films.title: The Truman Show
films.note: Another entry in Rudin's résumé of decades of hits.

films.title: The Royal Tenenbaums
films.note: Cited among Rudin's credits.

films.title: No Country for Old Men
films.note: The Oscar winner rounding out Rudin's list of hits.

films.title: Steve Jobs
films.note: Sorkin's screenplay almost got made at Sony; it fell apart amid the Pascal-Rudin arguments that then leaked in the hack, and the film moved to Universal.

segments.heading: What the emails revealed about the movies
segments.note: how films get made, and killed

films.title: American Hustle
films.note: The Daily Beast found Jennifer Lawrence's back-end pay was less than her male co-stars'; Amy Adams, a four-time Oscar nominee, also got less. The revelation fed a wider pay-equity conversation that ran through Patricia Arquette's 2015 Oscar speech and Lawrence's Lenny essay.

films.title: After Earth
films.note: Ben Fritz's favorite find was the marketing memo laying out how to hide that M. Night Shyamalan directed it and that Will Smith 'isn't really in this movie.' Sony spent over $130 million on it.

films.title: Concussion
films.note: The New York Times found emails showing 'unflattering moments' for the NFL were cut from an early script of the Will Smith drama, with a lawyer taking out 'most of the bite.'

films.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
films.note: The letdown that had Sony scrambling to fix its franchise; the leaked documents held a Sony-Marvel agreement requiring Peter Parker to be white and straight, plus early talk of bringing Spider-Man into the MCU.

segments.heading: The Obama emails
segments.note: the exchange that pulled in Al Sharpton

films.title: Django Unchained
films.note: Ahead of an Obama breakfast, Pascal emailed Rudin guessing the president liked films with Black leads: 'Should I ask him if he liked Django?' BuzzFeed's publication of the exchange drew a rebuke from Zoe Saldana and a sit-down with Al Sharpton.

films.title: The Butler
films.note: Another title Pascal floated in the same Obama email.

films.title: Think Like a Man
films.note: The third film Pascal named, before Rudin replied, 'I bet he likes Kevin Hart.'

Unmade: Men in Black / Jump Street crossover
Podcasts: Do We Get to Win This Time?
